Regulation: ministry of infrastructure and water management
Operations: ministry of infrastructure and water management

regulation.label: Regulation
keywords: ministry of infrastructure and water management

operations.label: Operations
keywords: ministry of infrastructure and water management

Damen Shipyards Group.description: One of the world's largest shipbuilding groups. Builds tugs, offshore vessels, dredgers, patrol vessels and ferries from a portfolio of 180+ standardised designs.

Caterpillar Marine.description: Marine division of Caterpillar Inc, supplying diesel and gas engines, propulsion systems and power solutions for commercial and government vessels.

Sanmar Shipyards.description: Turkish shipyard specialising in tugboat design and construction. Builds Robert Allan-designed tugs and supplies operators worldwide including major European port operators.

Kongsberg Maritime.description: Technology provider for maritime industries, delivering automation, navigation, simulation and dynamic positioning systems. Part of Kongsberg Gruppen ASA.

Bureau Veritas.description: International classification society and certification body. Provides conformity assessment, testing, inspection and certification for the marine industry.

W\u00e4rtsil\u00e4.description: Global technology group serving the marine and energy markets. Supplies engines, propulsion systems, energy storage and LNG solutions.

DNV.description: International classification society and assurance provider. Risk management, certification and technical advisory for maritime, oil & gas and energy sectors.

Lloyd's Register.description: Classification society providing independent assurance to companies operating high-risk, capital-intensive assets across maritime and offshore industries.

American Bureau of Shipping.description: Classification society for commercial vessels and offshore platforms. Sets technical standards for design, construction and operation of marine vessels.

Schottel.description: Manufacturer of propulsion and manoeuvring systems for ships. Produces rudder propellers, thrusters, tunnel thrusters and steerable systems for tugs and offshore vessels.

Voith.description: Technology company supplying Voith Schneider Propellers and other drive systems widely used in tugs and ferries requiring high manoeuvrability.

Hempel.description: Global manufacturer of protective coatings for marine, protective, decorative and container segments. Major supplier of antifouling and hull coatings.

Lankhorst Ropes.description: Dutch manufacturer of synthetic fibre ropes for marine and offshore applications, including mooring lines and towing equipment.

Shell Nederland.description: Dutch subsidiary of Shell plc. Operates refinery operations and fuel supply in the Netherlands, a key bunker fuel supplier for port operations.

BP Raffinaderij Rotterdam.description: BP's Rotterdam refinery operation. Major fuel supplier for maritime operations in the ARA (Amsterdam-Rotterdam-Antwerp) region.

RWE Renewables.description: Offshore wind division of RWE AG. Operates and develops offshore wind farms in the North Sea and Irish Sea, requiring specialist marine support.

\u00d8rsted.description: Danish state-controlled energy company and the world's largest offshore wind developer. Operates major wind farms in the North Sea including Hornsea.

SSE Renewables.description: Renewable energy division of SSE plc. Develops and operates offshore and onshore wind farms, including Dogger Bank offshore wind project.

TotalEnergies.description: French multinational integrated energy company active in offshore oil, gas and renewables. Operates platforms and vessels in the North Sea.

DEME Group.description: Belgian dredging and offshore contractor. Specialises in marine infrastructure, offshore energy installation and environmental works.

Jan De Nul.description: Belgian dredging, land reclamation and offshore contractor. One of the world's largest dredging companies, operating in ports and offshore wind markets.

Boskalis.description: Leading global dredging, offshore and marine services contractor. Also operates SMIT Salvage and provides towage, salvage and wreck removal services.

Van Oord.description: Dutch marine contractor specialising in dredging, offshore wind and marine engineering. A key player in North Sea offshore wind installation projects.

ArcelorMittal.description: World's second-largest steel and mining company. Operates Gent and IJmuiden steel plants in the Benelux, generating bulk cargo flows through Zeeland ports.

Equinor.description: Norwegian state-owned energy company active in oil, gas and offshore wind. Operates offshore platforms in the North Sea requiring marine support services.

Yara International.description: Norwegian chemical company and world's largest fertiliser producer. Operates bulk terminals at Sluiskil (Terneuzen) and Brunsbüttel, generating regular bulk shipping movements.

ExxonMobil.description: American multinational oil & gas company. Operates Esso refinery at Rotterdam and chemical complex at Botlek, generating tanker traffic through Maasvlakte.

Tata Steel.description: Steel producer with major IJmuiden plant in the Netherlands. One of the largest industrial bulk cargo generators in the North Sea port range.

INEOS.description: British multinational chemical company. Operates large petrochemical plants at Antwerp and Grangemouth, generating significant tanker and bulk traffic.

Cargill.description: American agricultural commodity trader. Operates grain and feed terminals at Ghent and Antwerp, generating dry bulk shipping movements.

Rijkswaterstaat.description: Dutch Directorate-General for Public Works and Water Management. Manages major waterways, ports of Rotterdam and Amsterdam infrastructure, and commissions dredging and maintenance contracts.

Sarens.description: Belgian heavy lift and engineered transport specialist. Moves large industrial cargo — including offshore wind components — through Zeeland and Antwerp ports.

DFDS.description: Danish ferry and freight shipping company. Operates ro-ro and passenger ferry routes across the North Sea and Channel, including routes to/from Zeeland ports.

Maersk Tankers.description: Product tanker company managing a fleet of refined oil tankers. Operates vessels calling at Rotterdam, Flushing and other ARA terminals.

Oldendorff Carriers.description: One of the world's largest dry bulk operators. Regularly calls at Zeeland Seaports and Ghent with coal, grain and other bulk cargoes.

Verbrugge Terminals.description: Zeeland-based terminal and logistics operator. Handles bulk cargo, breakbulk and project cargo through Vlissingen and Terneuzen terminals — a key port partner for Multraship.

Boluda Towage.description: Spanish maritime group and one of Europe's largest towage operators. Expanded into Northwest Europe through acquisition of Smit Towage assets.

Svitzer.description: Global towage and marine services company. Operates in 30+ countries with 450+ vessels. Historically a Maersk subsidiary; direct competitor in Northwest European ports.

Kotug International.description: Dutch towage company operating in Rotterdam, Europoort and European ports. Also active in offshore support and ship-to-ship transfer operations.

SMIT Salvage.description: International salvage and wreck removal company, part of the Boskalis group. Competes with Multraship in offshore salvage and emergency response.

Buks\u00e9r og Berging.description: Norwegian towage and salvage operator. Active in Scandinavian ports and competes in international salvage tenders.

Resolve Marine.description: American marine salvage, wreck removal and emergency response company. International competitor in casualty response contracts.

Fairplay Towage.description: Rotterdam-based harbour towage and terminal support provider. Operates in Dutch and Belgian ports.

Ardent.description: Marine salvage joint venture between Svitzer and Crowley Maritime. Competes directly with Multraship in offshore wreck removal and casualty management.

SAAM Towage.description: Chilean towage company with operations across the Americas and expanding into European markets. Growing global competitor.

Tsavliris Salvage.description: Greek maritime salvage group with over 70 years of experience. Active in Mediterranean and international salvage operations.

Iskes Towage.description: Dutch towage and salvage company operating from IJmuiden. Active in the Amsterdam port range and offshore areas of the North Sea.

Bugsier.description: German harbour towage and salvage company. Operates in Hamburg, Bremen and other German ports; member of the OCEAN network of independent towage companies.

Alfons H\u00e5kans.description: Finnish towage and icebreaking company. Dominant operator in Finnish ports; also active in Baltic Sea towage and wreck removal.
